factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, as restructuring actions have reduced expenses without significant margin pressure.

evidence.text: The decrease in SG&A expenses in the third quarter and first nine months of 2019 was primarily due to lower advertising and selling expenses in China. We incurred pre-tax restructuring and impairment expenses of $6.7 million in the first quarter of 2018, primarily related to employee severance and compensation-related costs, building lease exit costs, and the impairment of assets.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2019 COMPARED TO 2018

factors.summary: Restructuring and cost reduction programs have delivered measurable savings, with SG&A expenses decreasing year-over-year.

evidence.text: Selling, general and administrative (SG&A) expenses in the third quarter and first nine months of 2019 decreased by $5.3 million and $32.0 million, respectively, as compared to the prior year periods. The decrease in SG&A expenses in the third quarter and first nine months of 2019 was primarily due to lower advertising and selling expenses in China. We believe that the measures of adjusted earnings, adjusted EPS, and adjusted segment earnings provide useful information to investors about our performance and allow management and our investors to better compare our performance period over period.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, particularly in North America, driven by higher volumes and lower steel costs.

evidence.text: The higher segment earnings and segment margin in the third quarter of 2019 compared to 2018 were primarily a result of higher water heater and boiler volumes, lower steel costs, and improvement in the profitability of our water treatment products, which added incremental profits from the Water-Right acquisition.

evidence.text: Water-Right, acquired on April 8, 2019, added approximately $16 million and $30 million to sales in the third quarter and first nine months of 2019, respectively. The higher segment earnings and segment margin in the third quarter of 2019 compared to 2018 were primarily a result of higher water heater and boiler volumes, lower steel costs, and improvement in the profitability of our water treatment products, which added incremental profits from the Water-Right acquisition.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated strong pricing power, particularly in the North America segment, which has contributed to revenue growth and sales projections. Despite some volume challenges in other regions, the pricing actions taken in mid-2018 have been effective in driving growth in both water heater and boiler sales.

evidence.text: we project our water heater sales will grow in 2019 compared to 2018 primarily due to mid-2018 pricing actions on water heaters related to steel and other inflationary costs. We expect boiler sales will grow five percent in 2019, driven by the continuing U.S. industry transition to higher efficiency products and our introduction of new products.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

evidence.text: The increase in sales in the third quarter 2019 compared to the same period last year was primarily due to higher volumes of water heaters, boilers, and water treatment products, which benefited from $16 million of Water-Right sales. The higher segment sales in the first nine months of 2019 were primarily due to mid-2018 pricing actions and higher volumes of boilers and water treatment products, which were partially offset by lower volumes of residential water heaters.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2019 COMPARED TO 2018

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin products in North America, driven by the acquisition of Water-Right and the new supply contract with Lowe’s, which is expected to increase sales significantly. However, in China, the revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin products, negatively impacting margins due to weak consumer demand and inventory build in the sales channel.

evidence.text: we continued to expand our North America water treatment platform by being named exclusive supplier of water treatment products to Lowe’s, with sales commencing in August 2018, and acquiring the Water-Right group of companies (Water-Right) in April 2019. We expect sales of North America water treatment products to increase by approximately 60 percent in 2019, compared to 2018, primarily due to sales of Water-Right products from the date of acquisition, a full year of sales to Lowe’s and volume growth.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

evidence.text: The higher segment earnings and segment margin in the third quarter of 2019 compared to 2018 were primarily a result of higher water heater and boiler volumes, lower steel costs, and improvement in the profitability of our water treatment products, which added incremental profits from the Water-Right acquisition. The decrease in China sales in the third quarter and first nine months of 2019 compared to the same periods last year was primarily due to weak consumer demand and inventory build in the sales channel that occurred in the first half of 2018.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2019 COMPARED TO 2018

factors.summary: Overall demand is decelerating, particularly in China and the Rest of World segment, due to weak consumer demand and elevated channel inventory levels. The company expects a significant decline in China sales, with a 23 percent decrease in U.S. dollars and 19 percent in local currency, compounded by the expectation of continued elevated channel inventory levels affecting consumer purchasing behavior. Additionally, the Chinese currency is anticipated to depreciate by approximately five percent in 2019 compared to 2018, further impacting demand.

evidence.text: We expect China sales to decline in 2019 at a rate of approximately 23 percent in U.S. dollars and approximately 19 percent in local currency due to inventory build in the sales channel that occurred in the first half of 2018 and an expectation that customers will scale back their purchases through the fourth quarter of 2019 due to continued elevated channel inventory levels. We believe Chinese consumer demand will continue to be weak and the Chinese currency will depreciate compared to the U.S. dollar by approximately five percent in 2019 compared with 2018.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

evidence.text: Sales in the third quarter of 2019 were $728 million or approximately three percent lower than sales of $754 million in the third quarter of 2018. Sales in the first nine months of 2019 were $2,242 million or approximately six percent lower than sales of $2,375 million in the same period last year. Our sales decline in the third quarter and first nine months of 2019 compared to the same periods in the previous year was primarily a result of lower sales in China due to weak consumer demand and elevated channel inventory levels.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2019 COMPARED TO 2018

factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed organic growth across its segments, with North America showing strong growth, particularly in water heater and boiler sales driven by pricing actions and product innovation, while the Rest of World segment, especially China, is facing a significant decline due to inventory issues and reduced demand.

evidence.text: In our North America segment, we project our water heater sales will grow in 2019 compared to 2018 primarily due to mid-2018 pricing actions on water heaters related to steel and other inflationary costs. We expect boiler sales will grow five percent in 2019, driven by the continuing U.S. industry transition to higher efficiency products and our introduction of new products.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

factors.summary: Management has provided forward guidance indicating a decline in consolidated sales for 2019, both in U.S. dollar and local currency terms. The expected decline is approximately five percent in U.S. dollar terms and approximately 3.5% in local currency terms. The challenges in China, including weak consumer demand and elevated channel inventory levels, are the primary drivers behind this expected decline. Specifically, China sales are projected to decrease by 23% year over year in U.S. dollar terms and 19% in local currency terms.

evidence.text: Combining all of these factors, we expect our consolidated sales to decline approximately five percent in U.S. dollar terms and approximately 3.5 percent in local currency terms in 2019.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

evidence.text: We expect our consolidated sales to decline approximately five percent in U.S. dollar terms and approximately 3.5% in local currency terms in 2019 due to prolonged headwinds in China, attributable to weak consumer demand, and elevated channel inventory levels. We expect full year China sales to be down 23% year over year in U.S. dollar terms and 19% in local currency terms.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2019 COMPARED TO 2018

factors.summary: Margins are under pressure due to rising input costs, currency depreciation in China, and lower sales volumes, leading to compression in both gross and operating margins. However, pricing actions may help mitigate some of these effects.

evidence.text: we project our water heater sales will grow in 2019 compared to 2018 primarily due to mid-2018 pricing actions on water heaters related to steel and other inflationary costs. We believe Chinese consumer demand will continue to be weak and the Chinese currency will depreciate compared to the U.S. dollar by approximately five percent in 2019 compared with 2018.
evidence.subsection: OVERVIEW

evidence.text: Gross profit margin in the third quarter of 2019 of 39.0% was lower than the gross profit margin of 40.6% in the third quarter of 2018. Gross profit margin in the first nine months of 2019 of 39.5% was lower than gross profit margin of 40.8% in the first nine months of 2018. The lower gross profit margin in each period was primarily due to lower sales volumes in China. Segment margins in both the third quarter and first nine months of 2019 were lower than the same periods last year primarily due to the factors mentioned above.
evidence.subsection: THIRD QUARTER AND FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2019 COMPARED TO 2018

factors.summary: Management's confidence appears mixed. On one hand, they express caution regarding expected sales declines and challenges in the Rest of World segment, particularly China. On the other hand, they demonstrate confidence in their restructuring and acquisition strategies, providing non-GAAP measures to investors to better compare performance period over period.

evidence.text: Combining all of these factors, we expect our consolidated sales to decline approximately five percent in U.S. dollar terms and approximately 3.5 percent in local currency terms in 2019. We believe Chinese consumer demand will continue to be weak and the Chinese currency will depreciate compared to the U.S. dollar by approximately five percent in 2019 compared with 2018.

evidence.text: We are providing non-GAAP measures (adjusted earnings, adjusted earnings per share, and adjusted segment earnings) that exclude restructuring and impairment expenses. factors.summary: The company faces significant near-term risks, primarily driven by weak demand in China, currency depreciation, and elevated inventory levels. Additionally, there are challenges related to integrating the Water-Right acquisition and potential currency fluctuations that could impact performance.

evidence.text: we expect China sales to decline in 2019 at a rate of approximately 23 percent in U.S. dollars and approximately 19 percent in local currency due to inventory build in the sales channel that occurred in the first half of 2018 and an expectation that customers will scale back their purchases through the fourth quarter of 2019 due to continued elevated channel inventory levels. We believe Chinese consumer demand will continue to be weak and the Chinese currency will depreciate compared to the U.S. dollar by approximately five percent in 2019 compared with 2018.

evidence.text: We expect our consolidated sales to decline approximately five percent in U.S. dollar terms and approximately 3.5% in local currency terms in 2019 due to prolonged headwinds in China, attributable to weak consumer demand, and elevated channel inventory levels. We expect full year China sales to be down 23% year over year in U.S. dollar terms and 19% in local currency terms. This strategy is supported by increased sales and market penetration, as evidenced by the acquisition's contribution to revenue growth.
